This page contains sensitive content '''not suitable for all audiences'''. Readers including you might consider these topic(s) disturbing, anxiety-inducing, or inappropriate: '''Suicide, mental trauma.''' ---- ----
{{VoidedThemeV2}}
{{Audio|volume=2.5|file=Brokenglass.mp3|secrettext=broken glass on the floor|align=center|mono=yes}} {{Class Custom Image|header=Survival Difficulty: Class|class=Mu|safety=Dilapidated Sections|security=Radioactive Hazards|entity=Extreme Entity Count|hexagon=μ|bg-image=Not90again.png|text-color=white}} ---- But this level can also be.. ---- {{Class Custom Image|header=Survival Difficulty: Class|class=4Ψ - Psychological|safety=Unsafe|security=Unsecure|entity=Severe Mental Hazards|hexagon=Ψ|bg-image=Not90again.png|text-color=white}} '''Level 90''' is the '''91st''' level of '''[[The Backrooms]]'''. ==Description== {{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nope|file=Neverending90.webp|caption='''Celebrating a birthday for a child who will never arrive. Oh, how sad!'''
The dilapidated and rusty rooms of Level 90 were once alive.}} '''Level 90''' appears to be an infinite segmentation of small, cramped rooms made to resemble a child's birthday party. The appearance of each room differs significantly while keeping the same consistent layout. While the flooring, wallpaper, and ceilings differ, each room has the same dull yellow on its walls and the same red tinge on its carpets. The party rooms and the whole level itself appear to be very dark, with horrifying 8-bit audio playing in your ears. Audio tracks resembling cave noises will always be heard behind the wanderer. The wanderer will also feel something behind them, shaking menacingly. Nothing is there, '''most of the time'''. Party tables and chairs are thrown onto the floor, wallpaper can be peeled off, and paintings appear distorted, with pitch-black eyes and red pupils. TVs can be found across the rooms, playing unknown, static-like visuals and audio that sound similar to those of the well-known radio station: '''96.7 fm'''. Bizarre sounds, including beeps, random snippets of voices, and even audio from historical events like the Apollo 11 mission and the Sputnik I telemetry signal. {{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nope|file=tstandsforteddy.png|caption='''The child needs friends. Join the party!'''
Once a thriving business.}} Each square-shaped room connects to one or more other rooms via a small opening in the wall. However, these doors may be irregularly spaced and appear slightly above the ground. The rooms are normally empty; they may include one or more pieces of randomly placed furniture. Each space's walls are covered with disturbing pictures of characters from popular kids' shows, frequently shown in a deformed or warped style. It is unclear whether these drawings were manually drawn on '''Level 90's''' walls or are part of the level itself. Some rooms' ceilings are covered in two fluorescent light panels that create a faint hum. Many wanderers who have listened closely to ceilings have reported faint whispers from above. Throughout '''Level 90''', an unidentified source plays a short, and very detuned piano piece indefinitely, adding to the level's eerie atmosphere. Furthermore, this song has very loud echoes that reverberate throughout '''Level 90''' for long periods. {{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nope|file=Neverending902.webp|caption='''Spongebob's day of recokning.'''
What was used to rent playrooms for children.}} This song appears to be a mental hazard for wanderers, since listeners experience feelings of melancholy or neglect, as if they had been ignored their entire lives. After 5 minutes of experiencing this effect, you will get insecurity and anxiety, thereby making the song a cognitive hazard. After 25 minutes, the effect gets progressively worse, making you hate and distrust anyone. After a few hours, you won't trust anything anymore. Not even walls, floors, or ceilings. You crouch down in a corner and put your head on your knees, crying softly. After days, you will commit suicide in '''Level 90'''. The most disturbing aspect of the level is the condition of the murals. While the figures initially appear normal from a distance, closer examination reveals grotesque distortions in the paint. Faces possess exaggerated smiles, empty eye sockets, elongated limbs, and impossible anatomical features. Wanderers have reported that these abnormalities vary between rooms, and no two murals are exactly alike. Individuals who remain at the level for extended periods often feel as though the painted characters are watching them, though there is no evidence that the murals physically move. {{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nope|file=Abandoned-birthday-party-gonna-be-38-tomorrow-dont-v0-lyCWBY66jMpOs4fSY4NxCN1vXsCiRXSrVWx361ZTuuM.webp|caption='''Condemnation.'''
You witnessed something you didn't want to know.}} Mobile devices in the level have their own search engine called '''"Pseudosearch"'''. '''Pseudosearch''' is a search engine supposedly accessible from broken computers, CRT televisions, and certain toy electronic devices found throughout '''Level 90'''. Unlike normal '''[[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]''' search engines, '''Pseudosearch''' does not connect to the internet. Instead, it appears to retrieve information from unknown sources, often displaying memories, imaginary websites, and records that should not exist. '''Pseudosearch''' resembles a late-1990s web browser. It consists of bright primary colors, childish fonts, animated balloons, and cheerful clip-art decorations. A smiling mascot known only as '''Buddy''' appears in the corner of every page and occasionally delivers encouraging messages such as "You're having fun, right?" "Nobody forgot your birthday." "Search forever!" The search bar is labeled '''"What do you want to remember?"''' rather than '''"Search."''' The most popular search results will be mentioned after this sentence. '''"Memories:"''' Returns photographs, recordings, and diary entries from the wanderer's own life. These results become more inaccurate the longer the wanderer stays in '''Level 90'''. Searching '''"My childhood"''' may display birthday parties that never happened or images containing unknown people. '''"PartySites:"''' These websites resemble abandoned '''GeoCities''' pages filled with animated confetti and MIDI music. Most contain invitations to parties that have no listed address. Examples include: cake4u.net, friendz4ever.org, and partytime1999.com. Attempting to visit these pages from devices outside '''Level 90''' always results in errors. '''"Lost Searches:"''' Queries occasionally return information regarding deceased wanderers, forgotten levels, or events that have not yet occurred. Searching: '''"Where am I?"''' may simply make: '''"Still here."''' '''"Images:"''' Image results are often distorted photographs of '''Level 90''' rooms. Faces are blurred, decorations appear altered, and empty chairs are strangely common. Some researchers believe these images depict future sections of the level. '''"Buddy:"''' Buddy is the engine's mascot. It resembles a crude cartoon child with party hats for eyes and an unnaturally wide smile. Buddy never leaves the corner of the screen. Its messages become increasingly concerning over time: '''"Everyone came to your party." "They'll be here soon." "Don't close the browser." "We worked hard decorating." "You promised you'd stay."''' Fortunately, Buddy has never been observed manifesting physically. '''"The Final Page:"''' Wanderers who perform thousands of searches without leaving '''Level 90''' may eventually receive a single result titled: '''"Your Party Is Ready!"''' Opening this page causes the browser to freeze and display a photograph of a decorated room containing a single birthday cake and numerous empty chairs. The image changes each time it is viewed. Researchers have discovered that every photograph corresponds to a specific location within '''Level 90'''. No wanderer who deliberately searched for the room has ever returned.

==Events==
Event 1
''The Children's Song''
When '''The Children's Song''' begins, the voices will begin to fade away, leaving wanderers in an unsettling silence for a few seconds until the singing returns. The voices will slowly increase in loudness before reaching alarmingly high levels, commonly driving wanderers insane. The voices will be accompanied by loud laughter from both children and adults. As of now, the music has been fully documented and does not match any known tunes from either [[The Backrooms]] or [[The Frontrooms|Earth]]. The song goes as follows: ''Little Johnny had a birthday,
All his jolly friends came
But when the cake was set
Something was not the same

Little Johnny felt unwell, and so he promptly left
But when he came back, all his friends had little clefts in their neck!

Little Johnny ran and ran, oh so far away
But how far he ran, he could not run
From the inevitable decay

And so he ran to the woods, his face puffy and teary
He sat down, and cried so much until he grew weary
He cried and cried until he grew weary
He cried and cried until he grew weary
But alas, a man had came
The day was dawn, his face was gone
But ol' Johnny could not run

The man stood there, his position unchanging
He had no stare, but still he glared
Straight at little Johnny

By night's end, when all was quiet, there lied on a tree
Atop its branch, lay the beheaded face
Of poor little Johnny'' Unfortunately, almost nothing is known about the song, and it is still shrouded in mystery. '''The Children's Song''' lasts three minutes until '''Level 90''' returns to its normal state. However, it has the biggest impact on wanderers due to its purgatorial lyrics and completely unpleasant atmosphere.
Event 2
''The Distortion''
Unfortunately, there is no signs of when '''The Distortion''' will occur, as it happens at random and without warning. During this event, the level's paintings will begin to warp in look, often changing beyond recognition. The drawings will appear to change facial expressions, specifically between terror and depression. The children's singing becomes more out of tune, and the piano music begins to fade. Wanderers will feel a sudden rush of anxiety and may be tempted to crawl in a fetal position and whisper to themselves. Many wanderers have claimed that the images have been graved and burnt on their minds; some future aftereffects may include unease around drawings, difficulty of rationalization, et cetera. Unfortunately, [[Object 1|Almond Water]] cannot treat these effects.
==Entities== An extreme amount of entities can be found in '''Level 90''', like [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 2|Windows]], [[Sanguine Festivus]], [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], [[Entity 5|Clumps]], [[Entity 7|Dullers]], and [[Entity 9|Facelings]]. Three level-exclusive entities are in the level, which will be mentioned below: ===The Painted Ones=== '''The Painted Ones''' are believed to be manifestations connected to murals. They appear to be black silhouettes that stand in the dark, and mimic the voices of famous characters. Encountering one often makes nearby paintings more distorted and unsettling. They are normally passive, but those who damage the murals have disappeared for unknown reasons, '''to never come back...''' ===Smearlings=== {{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nope|file=40px-End.webp|caption='''Static.'''
A dark past.}} '''Smearlings''' resemble humanoid figures composed of wet paint and plaster fragments. They emerge from damaged walls and abandoned corners, leaving trails of dark colors behind them. '''Smearlings''' avoid brightly lit areas and can be repelled by exposure to strong light. They are slow-moving but highly persistent. ===r2=== '''r2''' is a pitch-black human silhouette standing at about 13 feet tall. It has a slim, uniform body with arms pressed to the body and legs closed together. It has no visible features. It shakes erratically both when standing still and when moving. '''r2''' will appear with the sound you're hearing and begin to rapidly approach the wanderer. '''r2''' will pass through most objects it runs into, with the exception of some materials like brick and stone. While '''r2''' is chasing the wanderer, any attempt to talk will not work for unknown reasons. Every second, there is a 1% chance that the time of day will change to day or midnight. '''r2''' cannot be killed and kills the wanderer when attacked. It will disappear 40 seconds after spawning. ====He==== '''He''' is a rare variant of '''r2'''. '''He''' is a human silhouette standing at about 13 feet tall. He has a glowing, static texture on a slim, uniform body, with arms pressed to the body and legs drawn together. He shakes erratically both when standing still and when moving. '''He''' will appear on the ground and start to move very slowly towards the wanderer. His appearance will be accompanied by a sudden start of rain inside the rooms and a lightning strike at the place of his spawning area. Otherwise, his behavior is identical to '''r2's''' behavior. ==Colonies, Bases and Outposts== No settlements are known to exist within '''Level 90'''. The disturbing atmosphere and unpredictable nature of the murals have prevented long-term habitation. ==Addendum== {{Fullscreen |num=2 |show=> Open Recovered Addendum |hide=> Close Recovered Addendum |content={{Background|file=The void.png}}
[[File:Not90again.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
The following document was discovered at '''Level 90'''. It is a paper with many entries, most likely written by a child. It is unclear when these entries were written or who made them. However, analysis has revealed that the child may have relations with '''Level 90''' itself. Take note that the child slowly descends into madness as they write these entries, making reading the journal very hard to do. A horizontal rule separates each entry.
It's almost my birthday, and I'm so excited! All my friends are coming, and there's going to be delicious cake! Although I'm in the Backrooms, it's almost like I was at home! Father found a fun level that he says should be perfect for my big day, and I can't wait to see it! Although I have to be honest, every level scares me. I don't like the dark. I don't like being alone. I don't like tight spaces. I don't like creepy smiles. I don't like creepy smiles. I don't like creepy smiles. I don't like creepy smiles. No, they're all terrible! But people should be happy on their birthday, so I'm going to be happy! It's just like what my best friend Gabriel told me. "Smile, and the world smiles with you." It's funny how much joy smiling can bring! But creepy smiles are different. They are evil. They are no good, and they just want to eat you! No fun! So I'm going to bed to get some rest. My birthday is in two days!
I saw Father's level! It's perfect! The rooms are big enough to have my friends. It's big and empty. And I feel so calm when I'm in the level! I wonder what flavor the cake is going to be? Anyways, I have the invites, and I'm going to pass them out today! It's so cool how my friends are all across the Backrooms in their home levels! One is in [[Level 11]]. One is in [[Level 4]]. One is in [[Level 1]]. There are so many, I can't count them all!
It's my birthday! I love it! All my friends are here. The cake is so good! Even Mother showed up! It makes me sad that Mother doesn't pay attention to me much of the time. She just wanders around, staring off into space for no reason. Father tells me that she's been through many things, and I understand. I just wish Mother actually knew that she has a son who loves her. I have a stomachache, so I'll go to the bathroom.
They're smiling at me. They're smiling at me. They're smiling at me. They're smiling at me. They're smiling at me. They're smiling at me. They won't stop. They keep looking at me. They're dead. Why do they keep looking at me? They keep staring at me. Into me. Their eyes are cut open. Their necks are cut open. Their mouths are spread across their faces. I did not smile, but the world smiled at me. I look away. They are gone. The lights turn off. I hear something in the distance. I hope it's not a creepy [[Entity 3|Smiler]]. The paintings and cartoons look weird. Where is Mom? Where is Dad? Someone... help me...
}} ==Thresholds== ===Entrances=== *Entrances to '''Level 90''' take on the form of a red door marked with a white balloon; the door may be adorned with party decorations, primarily glitter or confetti. Entering this door can lead a wanderer into a party/game-themed level like [[Level 86]] or [[Level Fun]]. *Dying to a [[Level Fun|Partygoer]] in [[Level Fun]] has a chance to bring the wanderer here. This chance is unknown since only one documented record of this has happened. *A party-themed section or a glowing staircase in [[Level 89]] will send one to '''Level 90'''. *Climbing up 90 stairs in total in [[Level 900]] will make the level transform into '''Level 90'''. *A black door marked with "PARTY AVAILABLE" in [[Level 11]] or [[Level 66]] will send one here. If it's marked with black balloons, then you will enter '''Level 90''' during the '''Darkening Effect'''. ===Exits=== *Exits to the level, which lead to other random levels, appear as black doors marked "EXIT".
*Finding a painting of a sunny tropical view will send you to [[Level 87]].
*If your watch ever stops working, you'll end up in [[Level 80]]..
